name: Regional Comprehensive Economic Partnership
climate: Tropical equatorial to temperate; monsoon-dominated in Southeast and East Asia, oceanic in Australasia

neighbors.name: ASEAN
neighbors.relevance: ASEAN is the founding bloc and institutional anchor of RCEP

neighbors.name: CPTPP
neighbors.relevance: Overlapping membership and competing trade liberalization framework in Asia-Pacific

neighbors.name: Indo-Pacific
neighbors.relevance: RCEP membership largely coincides with the Indo-Pacific strategic region

neighbors.name: South China Sea
neighbors.relevance: Key maritime trade corridor connecting RCEP economies; disputes affect member relations

neighbors.name: Strait of Malacca
neighbors.relevance: Primary shipping lane for intra-RCEP trade between East Asia and Southeast Asia/Oceania

neighbors.name: Taiwan Strait
neighbors.relevance: Critical semiconductor supply chain corridor; Taiwan not an RCEP member but deeply integrated

links.description: ASEAN's 10 members form the institutional core that initiated and hosts RCEP
Seven nations belong to both RCEP and CPTPP, creating overlapping trade liberalization
Three Quad members (AUS, JPN, IND observer) are RCEP parties, blending security with trade
China uses RCEP to complement BRI economic integration across Asia-Pacific
Maritime trade routes through the South China Sea carry the bulk of intra-RCEP shipping
Energy and goods transit between RCEP's Northeast and Southeast Asian members
RCEP membership substantially overlaps with Indo-Pacific strategic frameworks
